chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI SMA No.34 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________ Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ For hearing of main petition. 21.08.2024 Syed Hassan Jafry, advocate for the petitioner. Per order dated 21.08.2023 read with Reports / Orders dated 11.05.2023, 01.06.2023, 10.08.2023 and 26.03.2024 of the Deputy Registrar (OS) (“Reports”), he has satisfied himself that all requisites to the hearing of the main petition, including its publication, have been fulfilled and no objection thereto has been received, therefore, the main petition shall be determined in terms herein. This is a petition under Sections 278 and 372 of Succession Act, 1925, praying for Letters of Administration and Succession Certificate in respect of the estate of late Mian Riffat Baqar (“Deceased”), who passed away intestate at Karachi on 15.05.2021. Per the Report, the Deceased is survived by the legal heirs mentioned in the Report and the death certificate, and the family registration certificate are recorded and having been examined by the Deputy Registrar. The estate of the deceased comprises of moveable and immovable properties mentioned in the Reports. The Reports record that the title documents in respect of the estate have been seen/verified by the Deputy Registrar / Nazir. The Report also catalogues that publication in respect hereof was undertaken in daily Jang, Karachi and Quetta on 20.04.2023 and that no objection of any sort whatsoever has been received in this regard. The presence of petitioner, legal heirs of deceased as well as witnesses was exempted vide order dated 21.08.2023 and in the said order the two witnesses have confirmed the contents of their affidavits filed herein.

The Report also catalogues that publication in respect hereof was undertaken in daily Jang, Karachi and Quetta on 20.04.2023 and that no objection of any sort whatsoever has been received in this regard. The presence of petitioner, legal heirs of deceased as well as witnesses was exempted vide order dated 21.08.2023 and in the said order the two witnesses have confirmed the contents of their affidavits filed herein. In view of the foregoing, it appears that all the requisites have been completed to the satisfaction of the Deputy Registrar and there appears to be no impediment to the grant hereof, therefore, this petition is hereby allowed. The office is directed to issue letters of administration, in respect of the immovable properties recorded in the Reports, and succession certificate, in respect of the moveable properties recorded in the Reports, as per Rules. Learned counsel submits on oral motion that the petitioner may be permitted to deposit the original/verified title documents of the immovable property as security for the grant of the letters of administration as it would be virtually impossible to secure the said instrument through any other means. The request appears to be reasonable and is hereby granted. This petition stands allowed in terms herein contained. Judge Khuhro/PA
